name: Weekday
description: Useful days of the week, typically for work and school

name: Weekend
description: Days of rest and leisure (Saturday and Sunday in the U.S.)

name: Holiday
description: Official days to celebrate specific events, national or cultural

name: bimester
description: A period of two months

name: Trimester
description: A period of three months, often used in schools or pregnancy

name: Quarter
description: A period of three months, often used in business or finance

name: Semester
description: Half of a school year, usually about six months

name: Year
description: A full calendar year, 12 months or 365 days (366 in leap years)

name: Decade
description: A period of ten years

name: Century
description: A period of one hundred years

name: Millennium
description: A period of one thousand years

name: Monday
description: The first day of the standard U.S. workweek

name: Tuesday
description: The second day of the standard workweek

name: Wednesday
description: Midweek, often called 'hump day'

name: Thursday
description: The fourth day of the workweek, nearing the weekend

name: Friday
description: Last day of the workweek for most, start of the weekend for some

name: Saturday
description: Weekend day, usually for rest and leisure

name: Sunday
description: Weekend day, often for rest or family activities

name: New Year's Day
description: January 1st, celebrating the start of the new year

name: Martin Luther King Jr. Day
description: Third Monday in January, honoring civil rights leader MLK Jr.

name: Presidents' Day
description: Third Monday in February, honoring U.S. presidents

name: Memorial Day
description: Last Monday in May, honoring fallen military members

name: Juneteenth
description: June 19th, celebrating the end of slavery in the U.S.

name: Independence Day
description: July 4th, celebrating U.S. independence

name: Labor Day
description: First Monday in September, honoring workers and labor movement

name: Columbus Day
description: Second Monday in October, recognizing Columbus’ arrival in the Americas

name: Veterans Day
description: November 11th, honoring all U.S. military veterans

name: Thanksgiving
description: Fourth Thursday in November, celebrating gratitude and family

name: Christmas
description: December 25th, celebrating the birth of Jesus Christ (widely observed)

name: Spring
description: March to May, a season of renewal and growth

name: Summer
description: June to August, often warm and sunny, vacation time

name: Autumn
description: September to November, also called fall, leaves change color

name: Winter
description: December to February, often cold, sometimes snowy

name: Early morning
description: The hours just after midnight, before sunrise

name: Morning
description: The hours after sunrise until noon

name: Noon
description: Around 12:00 PM, the middle of the day

name: Afternoon
description: The hours after noon until early evening

name: Evening
description: The hours from late afternoon until night

name: Night
description: The hours after sunset, often for rest

name: Midnight
description: 12:00 AM, the very start of a new day

name: Breakfast
description: The first meal of the day, usually in the morning

name: Brunch
description: A late morning meal, between breakfast and lunch

name: Lunch
description: A meal in the middle of the day

name: Afternoon snack
description: A small snack in the mid-afternoon

name: Dinner
description: The main evening meal

name: Late-night meal
description: Food eaten late at night, sometimes after social activities

name: Coffee break
description: A short pause, usually mid-morning or mid-afternoon, to refresh with coffee or a snack

name: Recess
description: A short break during school or work for rest or play

name: Lunch break
description: A longer pause around noon for eating and relaxing

name: Siesta
description: Optional rest in early afternoon (rare in the U.S., more common in other cultures)

name: Power nap
description: A short daytime sleep to recharge energy, usually 10–30 minutes

name: Study/work session
description: A focused period of work or study, often followed by a short break
